method: Horizontal bar chart with encoded logic from V1. Y-axis: Course.CName sorted by enrollment count. X-axis: Student count. Color: Department.Division (AS/EN). Tooltips show exact counts and department names.
objective: Visually compare enrollment distribution across divisions to identify departmental enrollment trends

method: Sorted bar chart showing top 10/bottom 10 courses by enrollment count. Use diverging color scale (red for low, green for high). Add reference line for average enrollment.
objective: Identify outlier courses with exceptionally high/low enrollment for further investigation

method: Stacked bar chart per V2. X-axis: Binned time categories (e.g., 'Morning-MTW'). Y-axis: Enrollment counts. Stack colors represent Days-Hours combinations. Interactive filters for time categories.
objective: Analyze temporal patterns influencing enrollment by comparing session timing popularity

method: Box plots per V3. X-axis: Division (AS/EN). Y-axis: Student age. Overlay jittered points for raw data. Statistical summary annotations (median, IQR).
objective: Determine if age demographics differ between divisions, potentially affecting course preferences

method: Scatter plot with course enrollment counts on x-axis and average gradepoints (calculated from Gradeconversion) on y-axis. Size encodes total enrollments. Color shows Department.Division.
objective: Explore relationship between course popularity and academic performance metrics

method: Bar chart showing average enrollment count per credit value (1-4). Error bars show standard deviation. Overlay actual enrollment counts as semi-transparent points.
objective: Associate credit hours with enrollment popularity to identify student workload preferences

method: Heatmap showing frequency of students enrolling in courses from their advisor's department. X-axis: Advisor's department (DNO). Y-axis: Course department (DNO). Color intensity: Enrollment count.
objective: Identify advising influence on course selection patterns
